name: Lee Bank
description_short: Community bank serving the Berkshires region with FDIC insurance, offering personal and business checking, savings, mortgages, and lending products with local decision-making.
description_long: Lee Bank is a community-focused financial institution headquartered in the Berkshires region of Massachusetts, serving customers across multiple local branches including Great Barrington, Stockbridge, Pittsfield, and Lenox. The bank is FDIC-insured and member of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, providing deposit protection backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Their stated mission emphasizes empowering employees, customers, and the community through local banking with service, sincerity, and simplicity. Lee Bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services. On the personal side, they provide multiple checking account options (EMPOWER Rewards, Kasasa Cash, Basic Checking), savings products (EMPOWER Rewards Savings, Kasasa Saver, Money Market), CDs, IRAs, HSAs, and personal credit cards. Their lending portfolio includes home mortgages, HELOCs, construction loans, auto loans, and personal loans. For business customers, they offer commercial checking accounts, business savings and money market accounts, business credit cards and debit cards, and specialized lending including SBA loans, small business digital loans up to $250,000, lines of credit up to $150,000, and commercial mortgages. What distinguishes Lee Bank is their emphasis on local decision-making and personalized service. They highlight quick online loan applications with local approval decisions, video banking capabilities, and same-day account opening. The bank also maintains a foundation providing community grants (awarding $48,800 in their 2025 fourth grant cycle) and offers financial wellness services through October Mountain Financial Advisors. Their recent security enhancements show modernization efforts, including migration to email verification codes for online banking. As a regional community bank, Lee Bank is appropriate for customers prioritizing local relationships and decision-making over national scale. However, customers should note they are geographically limited to the Berkshires region, which may restrict branch access for those outside Massachusetts. Their product offerings are standard for community banks but may lack the specialized digital features or competitive rates of larger national institutions or fintech alternatives.
services: Free Kasasa Cash and Kasasa Cash Back checking options
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home mortgage loans, HELOCs, and construction loans
Small business digital loans up to $250,000 and lines of credit up to $150,000
Online and video banking with email-verified security
Identity theft protection and secure email services
Financial advisory services through October Mountain Financial Advisors
pros: FDIC-insured deposits providing federal protection up to applicable limits
Local decision-making on loans with quick approval processes advertised
Small business lending available up to $250,000 with dedicated support
Multiple account types including rewards-based checking and Kasasa products
Video banking and video call scheduling options for remote account management
Community focus with active foundation providing $48,000+ in annual grants
Online account opening capability described as opening accounts in minutes
cons: Geographic limitation to Berkshires region (Great Barrington, Stockbridge, Pittsfield, Lenox area) restricts accessibility for non-local customers
No information provided about fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or interest rates on their website
Limited evidence of mobile app functionality or modern digital banking features compared to national competitors
Small regional institution means potentially less competitive rates and fewer ATM/branch locations than major banks
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or advanced financial planning services beyond basic advisory
best_for: Small business owners in the Berkshires seeking local lending relationships and personalized service
Residents of western Massachusetts communities wanting community-focused banking with local decision-makers
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and traditional banking services over digital-first alternatives
Individuals seeking mortgages, HELOCs, or construction financing with local underwriting
diagnosis: Lee Bank is best suited for Berkshires-area residents and small business owners seeking traditional community banking with local relationships and FDIC insurance protection. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the Massachusetts Berkshires region will have restricted branch access, and the website lacks transparent pricing, rates, and fee information necessary for competitive comparison with regional and national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Lee Bank serves the Berkshires with FDIC insurance, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and lending products.
